text: NO CHINA DEAL: Trump returned from Beijing May 16-17 with zero new Iran commitments from Xi Jinping despite claiming China agreed Hormuz 'must reopen' and Iran shouldn't get nukes. Analysts note these were China's pre-existing public positions — not concessions. Trump posted 'to be continued!' on Truth Social, signaling military options remain live. Iran FM Araghchi said at BRICS New Delhi: 'We are in doubt about their seriousness' regarding US intentions. The diplomatic window is narrowing rapidly.
verificationNote: CNN confirmed Trump's return and Truth Social post May 16. Bloomberg confirmed China aligned positions were pre-existing. Araghchi BRICS quote confirmed by Fortune, Al Jazeera May 15-16.

text: ARAGHCHI WARNING: Iranian FM Araghchi posted on social media May 16 warning American households directly: 'Americans are told that they must absorb rocketing costs of war of choice on Iran.' He warned 'real pain begins when US debt and mortgage rates start to jump' — a deliberate attempt to use domestic US economic pain as diplomatic leverage. His message follows Rubio's rebuttal that Iran 'attempting to use US politics will not pressure President Trump into a bad deal.' Talks remain deadlocked.
verificationNote: Araghchi social media post confirmed by Al Jazeera May 16 article 'Iran warns of readiness for war and economic costs.' Rubio rebuttal confirmed by The Hill May 15-16.

text: HORMUZ PGSA: Iran's parliament security committee chair Ebrahim Azizi confirmed May 16 that Iran has prepared a 'professional mechanism to manage traffic in the Strait of Hormuz along a designated route, which will be unveiled soon.' The mechanism — called the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) — will charge up to $2M per vessel for transit approval, settled in Chinese yuan. US 'Project Freedom' ships are explicitly excluded. Iran is effectively seeking to commercialize the blockade. International maritime law experts warn this constitutes illegal extortion of commercial shipping.
verificationNote: Azizi PGSA announcement confirmed by PressTV, Times of Israel liveblog, Al Jazeera liveblog, Investing.com May 16. Toll amount confirmed by Kurdistan 24 and NPR.

text: SPR + OIL: DOE awarded contracts May 12-14 for 53.3 million barrel emergency SPR release: Trafigura (13M), Marathon (12.4M), ExxonMobil (11.4M). Total SPR drawdown since the war: ~80M barrels. Brent crude at $109.26/bbl (+36.9% from $79.84 pre-war); WTI $105.42 (+39.6% from $75.51). AAA national average: $4.52/gal. OPEC+ May 3 symbolic 188k b/d output hike offset by Hormuz closures. UAE formally left OPEC May 1. IEA warns market materially undersupplied through October even if war ends immediately.
verificationNote: SPR release confirmed by Al Jazeera May 12, NaturalNews/ABC10 May 14. OPEC+ hike confirmed by CNBC May 3. UAE OPEC exit confirmed by The National May 1. Oil prices confirmed by TradingEconomics/OilPrice.com May 15-17.

text: NCRI DC RALLY: Thousands of Iranian diaspora supporters rallied near the US Capitol on May 16 — the largest Iranian-American political demonstration of the war — organized by the National Council of Resistance of Iran (NCRI). Former Gen. Wesley Clark addressed the crowd. NCRI is positioning Maryam Rajavi's provisional government as the transitional authority for post-Islamic Republic Iran. The rally coincides with US-Israel strike preparation reports, representing a coordinated regime-change pressure campaign as ceasefire talks collapse.
verificationNote: NCRI rally confirmed by Fox News live updates May 16. NCRI press release confirmed from ncr-iran.org. Gen. Clark speech confirmed by Fox News reporting.

text: ESCALATION ALERT: Trump posted AI war imagery on Truth Social May 17 — himself ordering fire on Iranian vessels: 'It was the calm before the storm.' This is the clearest pre-strike signal Trump has sent since the April 8 ceasefire. Israeli media sources are reporting a 24-hour decision window for Trump to authorize resumed strikes on Iran. The window closes sometime May 17-18 US time.
verificationNote: Trump Truth Social posts confirmed by Times of Israel, IANS Live, Arab Times Online. 24-hour decision window reported by Times of Israel citing Israeli officials May 17.

text: PGSA FORMALIZED: Iran has officially unveiled the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) on May 17. Details: $2M per transit approval fee, payable in Chinese yuan. US 'Project Freedom' escort vessels explicitly excluded. Ships from 'cooperating nations' only. International maritime lawyers say this is illegal extortion. Trump has ordered the US Navy to destroy Iranian mines in the Strait and blockade any ship paying Iranian tolls. Dual blockade now fully institutionalized.
verificationNote: PGSA announcement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog, CBS News, The Hill May 17. Trump mine-clearing order confirmed by CBS News. $2M fee confirmed by Kurdistan 24 and NPR.

text: ARAGHCHI 'VICTOR' CLAIM: Iranian FM Araghchi declared on state TV May 17 that 'all countries acknowledge Iran was the victor in this war.' Simultaneous with this claim: Hezbollah launched a drone swarm at an IDF base in northern Israel, IDF intercepts rockets in south Lebanon, and Trump just posted 'calm before the storm' war imagery. Iran claiming victory while simultaneously escalating militarily and economically. Classic Iranian strategic communications doctrine.
verificationNote: Araghchi 'victor' statement confirmed by CBS News Iran war live Day 79. Hezbollah drone swarm and rocket intercept confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 17.

text: TRUMP 'VERY BAD TIME' WARNING: On BFM TV, Trump told Iran it would have 'a very bad time' if no deal is reached. He also said the US could destroy Iran's entire infrastructure 'in two days' — though CENTCOM Adm. Cooper told the Senate Iran has restored 30/33 Hormuz missile sites. Trump's threats + 'calm before the storm' posts + Israeli strike prep reports = a multi-signal escalation package that looks coordinated. Next 24-48 hours critical.
verificationNote: Trump BFM TV statement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog May 17, Iran International. '30/33 missile sites' confirmed by Euronews ISW assessment May 13.

text: OIL DAY 79 AM: Brent pushing toward $111-113 range on May 17 intraday as Trump escalation signals register in markets. WTI holding ~$106. The PGSA toll formalization plus Trump's 'very bad time' warning is structurally bearish for any near-term Hormuz reopening. Goldman Sachs updated Brent target to $120+ if strikes resume. IEA remains on record that the market is undersupplied through October 2026 regardless.
verificationNote: Intraday Brent range confirmed by Goodreturns.in ($113.21 intraday) and OilPrice API ($108.17). Goldman Sachs target confirmed by Bloomberg. IEA October undersupply confirmed by Fortune/IEA reports.

text: DAY 79 MORNING STATUS: Trump 'calm before storm' posts + 24hr strike decision window. Iran PGSA toll $2M/vessel in yuan formalized. Araghchi 'Iran won.' Hezbollah drone swarm at IDF N. Israel + rockets intercepted in S. Lebanon. Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days (May 15) but IDF still striking Tyre. Lebanon: 2,969 killed, 9,112 wounded. Israel: 50 killed. US: 13 KIA. Brent ~$111. Gas $4.52. Hormuz Blockade Day 76. Most volatile moment since April 8 ceasefire.
verificationNote: All figures compiled from Times of Israel May 17, Al Jazeera liveblog May 17, CBS News Iran war live, TradingEconomics oil prices, AAA gas prices.

text: UAE Federal Authority for Nuclear Regulation confirms no radiological release after drone struck electrical generator on outer perimeter of Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant. All four reactors continue operating normally. UAE: first attack on the facility since war began February 28.
verificationNote: Confirmed by UAE FANR official statement and The National reporting.

displayName: Institute for the Study of War
text: ISW analysis: Iran has restored operational status to 30 of 33 Hormuz-area missile launch sites degraded during Operation Epic Fury. IRGC Navy repositioning observed via satellite. Iran is in active preparation posture for potential resumption of hostilities — any resumed US-Israel campaign faces reconstituted defense networks.
verificationNote: ISW assessment per Euronews May 13 and CNN May 13 reporting.

text: CT/ISW analysis confirms Russian drone components are being shipped to Iran via Caspian Sea maritime route to rebuild Iran offensive drone capability depleted during Operation Epic Fury. This represents a major sanctions violation enabling Iranian military reconstitution during the ceasefire period.
verificationNote: Per Critical Threats Iran Update special report May 8-10.

text: USS Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group returned to Naval Station Norfolk after a 326-day deployment — the longest US carrier deployment since Vietnam War. The Ford brought approximately 5,000 sailors home. USS Abraham Lincoln and USS George H.W. Bush carrier strike groups remain on station in Arabian Sea.
verificationNote: Official US Fleet Forces Command press release.

text: Araghchi at BRICS FM meeting in New Delhi: "We have no trust in the US. The moment we feel they are serious and ready for a fair and balanced deal, we will proceed with negotiations." Described the nuclear enrichment deadlock as likely to be postponed to later negotiating stages. BRICS meeting ended without joint communique for second consecutive time.
verificationNote: Confirmed by NBC News and Al Jazeera May 15 coverage.

text: FBI and CISA investigating breach of internet-connected automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems at US gas stations across multiple states. Iranian-linked hackers exploited unpassworded online systems — no fuel manipulation confirmed but safety concerns raised (undetected gas leaks possible). Broader pattern: Tehran-linked hackers have disrupted US oil/gas, water, and logistics systems since February 28.
verificationNote: Per CNN Politics May 15 and Gulf News reporting. Attribution to Iran pending FBI forensic confirmation.

text: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'For Iran, the Clock is Ticking, and they better get moving, FAST, or there won't be anything left of them. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!' Posted after Trump-Netanyahu phone call as ceasefire deadline passed. Trump scheduling Situation Room meeting for Tuesday with Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, Ratcliffe to discuss military options against Iran.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Fox News, CBS News live updates, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera May 17 liveblog.

text: Netanyahu-Trump phone call on May 17: both sides coordinating on possible resumed military strikes on Iran. Netanyahu demanded enriched uranium removal and dismantlement of nuclear facilities as non-negotiable. Netanyahu convened limited security cabinet after call. IDF put on alert for 'surprise scenarios.' NYT reported US/Israel in 'intensive preparations' for possible resumed strikes as early as next week.
verificationNote: Trump-Netanyahu call confirmed by Times of Israel, ITV News, Jerusalem Post. IDF alert confirmed by Times of Israel.

text: IAEA Director General Grossi expressed 'grave concern' over the drone strike on the UAE's Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant perimeter — the first attack on any nuclear power plant in the war. IAEA sent a monitoring team to verify UAE's claim of no radiological release. Grossi called for 'immediate protection of all civilian nuclear facilities' under international humanitarian law.
verificationNote: IAEA response confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog May 17 citing IAEA Director General Grossi statement.

text: USTR Greer on ABC News 'This Week': 'Trump was very focused on making sure [China] didn't provide material support to Iran. That's a commitment he obtained and confirmed' from Xi Jinping at the Beijing summit. Greer also said China 'wants the Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions.' First official US confirmation of Xi's Iran commitment.
verificationNote: Confirmed by ABC News and KXLY reporting on Greer's May 17 ABC 'This Week' interview.

text: Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) toll unveiling confirmed for May 17-18. Vessels cooperating with Iran pay $1-2M per transit in yuan. US/Israel-linked vessels permanently barred. 40+ approval questions required. OFAC already warned non-US firms that paying Iran tolls triggers secondary sanctions. Hormuz traffic remains at ~5% of pre-war levels. 1,550+ vessels still stranded.
verificationNote: PGSA details confirmed by Maritime Executive, NPR (May 14), Investing.com, and Al Jazeera liveblog May 16-17.

text: Araghchi on Day 79: US 'failed to offer any real concessions.' Iran remains prepared to maintain ceasefire but 'also prepared to go back to fighting.' Separately, Araghchi warned UAE at BRICS: 'Enmity with the Great People of Iran is a foolish gamble. Collusion with Israel in doing so: unforgivable.' Barakah nuclear plant drone strike dramatically raised the stakes for UAE.
verificationNote: Mehr News/Al Jazeera May 17 liveblog. Araghchi UAE warning from BRICS confirmed by Euronews and Mehr News May 14.

text: CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper Senate testimony (May 14): Iran has attacked US forces more than 10 times since the April 7 ceasefire began. Iran's military capability is 'significantly degraded' and Iran 'no longer threatens regional partners or the US in ways they were able to before, across every domain.' Iran has restored 30 of 33 Hormuz-area missile sites per ISW. Two carrier strike groups remain on station.
verificationNote: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper testimony confirmed by CBS News and Al Jazeera May 14-15. ISW missile site restoration per Euronews May 13.

text: DAY 80 ESCALATION STACK: Barakah nuclear plant drone hit (first nuclear facility struck in war) + Saudi Arabia intercepts 3 drones from Iraqi airspace + Iran PGSA formally operational ($2M/transit in yuan) + Iran threatens Hormuz undersea cable fees + Hezbollah drone swarm on IDF northern Israel — all within 8 hours. Trump-Netanyahu call concluded; Situation Room meeting scheduled for May 19. The next 48-72 hours are the highest-risk window since April 8 ceasefire.
verificationNote: All individual events confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, Times of Israel liveblogs, Maritime Executive, CNN.

text: BARAKAH SIGNIFICANCE: The drone strike on UAE's Barakah nuclear plant is the first attack on any nuclear power facility in this war. UAE intercepted 2 of 3 drones — the third hit an external generator. IAEA DG Grossi condemned it and dispatched a monitoring team. No group claimed responsibility; western border approach vector points to Iran-linked proxies. This crosses a bright line in the laws of armed conflict regarding civilian nuclear installations.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, CNBC, The National UAE, Times of Israel liveblog. IAEA response confirmed.

text: PGSA OPERATIONAL: Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority is now formally collecting toll fees. OFAC secondary sanctions create an impossible bind for Western shippers — pay Iran and face US sanctions, or don't pay and can't transit Hormuz. Bloomberg reported ~2M bbl/day trickling through (4 Iraqi supertankers exited since May 10) vs 20M pre-war. IEA: market remains 'materially undersupplied through October' even if conflict ends now. The PGSA institutionalizes Iranian control of Hormuz beyond the war itself.
verificationNote: PGSA details confirmed by Maritime Executive, NPR May 14. Bloomberg oil flow data confirmed May 14. IEA assessment confirmed.

text: OIL MARKETS DAY 80: Brent pulled back slightly to ~$110.63 (-0.4% from Friday's $111 close) as markets digest escalation signals. WTI ~$105.42. Weekly gains: Brent +8.1%, WTI +11% (week ending May 16). Goldman Sachs updated Q4 Brent target to $90 IF conflict ends; raised to $120+ if strikes resume. Iran stock market set to reopen Tuesday May 19 — regime normalizing economic signals while maintaining military escalation posture.
verificationNote: Oil prices from TradingEconomics and oilprice.com. Iran stock market reopen confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog and Guru Focus.

text: ARAGHCHI DUAL-TRACK DAY 80: At BRICS New Delhi, Araghchi declared 'Iran won' and warned UAE that collusion with Israel is 'unforgivable' — hours after the Barakah nuclear plant drone strike. Simultaneously, Iran announced stock market reopens May 19 (regime stabilization signal). Classic Iranian dual-track: maximum pressure messaging externally while demonstrating domestic normalization. Iran is negotiating from a position it believes is strength — but Trump's Situation Room meeting May 19 may test that assumption.
verificationNote: Araghchi BRICS statement confirmed by Press TV, Al Jazeera. Stock market reopen confirmed by Times of Israel. Trump Situation Room meeting confirmed by Fox News, Axios.
